name: Saudi Arabia
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **A12 SAUDI FM PRINCE FAISAL BIN FARHAN CALLED IRANIAN FM ARAGHCHI AND PAKISTANI FM ISHAQ DAR SEPARATELY MAY 11 TO DISCUSS 'PAKISTAN'S MEDIATION EFFORTS.'** Pakistan formally still official mediator; Qatar substantive (consistent with May 10 Axios scoop on Rubio/Witkoff/al-Thani Miami meeting). The multi-channel architecture (Pakistan official + Qatar substantive + Saudi underwriter + Russia uranium-custodian standing offer) is now publicly visible. Pakistan source: 'no immediate danger' of war resumption; back-channels active despite public harshness. **B6 WSJ MAY 11 REVELATION REFRAMES SAUDI POSITIONING: UAE secretly struck Iran's Lavan refinery April 8 — Saudi visibly distancing from UAE's military track. Saudi public posture (operational deniability + public-pro-mediation) is now READING AS DELIBERATE DISTINCTION from UAE's only-third-combatant status.** The Saudi public denial via Al-Hadath May 8 (no offensive-airspace use) is more meaningful in light of WSJ Lavan revelation — it's not just a public-relations posture, it's an explicit positioning AGAINST UAE's military track. Qatari and Saudi MoFAs remained publicly silent on the Doha bulk carrier strike (Day 2 of 7-day trigger window). The 'Iran responds via Qatar-IRGC channel rather than Pakistan-FM channel within 7 days' trigger (added May 10) is at Day 2 of 7 — Iran's formal response on May 10-11 went via PAKISTAN per public reporting (no Qatar-IRGC channel attribution); substantive-mediator-via-Qatar trigger NOT yet confirmed by operational signal. **Aramco CEO Nasser May 11: Hormuz transit 2-5 ships/day (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June** — industry-CEO authority. Aramco runs the only adjacent-scale crude operation; Nasser is unlikely to overstate disruption which hurts his customer base. Tadawul (TASI) 11,158.45 (+0.4% 1D, -1.4% 1M, -0.1% 3M, essentially flat vs pre-crisis 11,170). World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman raised 2026 average to $85 (from $77) + JPM $60 = institutional band still threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even) but Goldman revision is bullish-marginal. Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED. — Bahrain detained 41 alleged IRGC-linked individuals May 9 (first major Gulf-state internal action against alleged IRGC network); Qatar formally re-emerged as parallel-track substantive mediator via IRGC senior generals (Axios scoop, Rubio/Witkoff/al-Thani Miami meeting Saturday); UAE air defense engagement May 8 confirmed 3 wounded.** Saudi public posture continues: operational deniability + public alignment with Pakistan-Iran de-escalation track. Saudi May 9 statement endorsing Pakistan's mediation (Radio Pakistan, Arab News) reinforces public-facing posture. **No Saudi MoFA statement on the Sunday bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike at filing time — despite Qatar being a fellow GCC member in same coastal waters.** The Gulf-state response to Iran is now openly bifurcated: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi Arabia (deniable-pro-US-operationally, public-pro-mediation), UAE (continues hawkish operationally), Qatar (now openly mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent mediator), Kuwait (denied airspace per WSJ scoop). PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Saudi Al-Hadath May 8 denial of WSJ offensive-airspace-lift scoop = MBS playbook (operational deniability + public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track). Tadawul +0.7% May 8 close (Friday partial trading day; Sunday open will be first reaction to Doha strike + Qatar mediator reframing). World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 = institutional forecast band threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even). Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED. Following Wednesday's WSJ scoop that Saudi Arabia and Kuwait had denied US base/airspace access for Project Freedom and that those restrictions had since been 'lifted,' Saudi officials briefed Washington's press on background while 'the Saudi government denies the story on the record' (CONFIRMED — The DuPree Report, Türkiye Today). An unnamed Saudi source told Saudi state-aligned broadcaster Al-Hadath: 'the Kingdom did not allow the use of its airspace to support offensive military operations' and Riyadh was 'supporting de-escalation and Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement' (CONFIRMED — Türkiye Today citing Al-Hadath primary). Iran International and Times of Israel separately note Saudi/Kuwait have lifted restrictions per US officials, but Saudi official line is now public denial on the record. **Structural read:** Saudi is doing two things simultaneously — (a) operationally permitting whatever US needs (per on-background US-officials lift narrative), (b) publicly DENYING any offensive support and aligning rhetorically with Pakistan-Iran de-escalation. This is the cleanest single example of MBS's playbook in cycle — operational deniability paired with public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track. The 'Saudi formally named primary mediator (or co-mediator) for US-Iran Hormuz channel within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)' trigger from qatar.json — if reframed for Saudi — is now within reach: Saudi has publicly endorsed Pakistan's mediation, which is itself a soft-mediator posture. Tadawul +0.7% May 8 (treated cautiously — Friday partial trading day; Sunday open will be first reaction to Al-Hadath denial). PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Saudi MBS extended silence on UAE OPEC exit; Tadawul -0.5% peace-dividend-bearish signal; Aramco fell 4.7% after-hours on UAE exit; UAE departure means ADNOC can undercut Saudi pricing. World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 = institutional forecast band threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even). Chatham House (May 2026): MBS posture 'strategic recalibration.' Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED.

active_triggers.condition: Yanbu pipeline capacity maxed out or sustained attack closes it
Sadara JV ($3.7B debt) defaults at June 15 grace expiry without operational recovery
Saudi formally named primary mediator (or co-mediator) for US-Iran Hormuz channel within 14 days (by 2026-05-22)
active_triggers.note: Day 1 of 14 as of 2026-05-09. Saudi public denial via Al-Hadath + endorsement of Pakistan mediation = soft proxy already; formal naming within 14 days would be the firmer signal.

recent_signals.headline: GULF-STATE BIFURCATION DEEPENS — BAHRAIN DETAINED 41 ALLEGED IRGC-LINKED MAY 9 (first major internal Gulf action vs IRGC network); QATAR FORMALLY RE-EMERGED AS PARALLEL-TRACK MEDIATOR VIA IRGC SENIOR GENERALS (Axios scoop, Rubio/Witkoff/al-Thani Miami Saturday); UAE AIR-DEFENSE ENGAGEMENT MAY 8 CONFIRMED 3 WOUNDED (UPDATED — yesterday underplayed scale: 2 ballistic + 3 drones); NO SAUDI MoFA STATEMENT ON SUNDAY BULK-CARRIER-NEAR-DOHA STRIKE DESPITE GCC FELLOW-MEMBER STATUS. Saudi May 9 endorsement of Pakistan's mediation via Radio Pakistan / Arab News reinforces public-facing posture. The Gulf bifurcation is now operationally specific: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi (deniable-pro-US-operationally + public-pro-mediation), UAE (hawkish operationally), Qatar (mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent), Kuwait (denied airspace). MBS playbook continues: operational deniability + public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track.
sources: Khaleej Times (Bahrain MoI primary, Saudi-region pickup)
Times of Israel May 9-10 live blogs
Radio Pakistan (Saudi endorsement of Pakistan mediation May 9)
recent_signals.verification: Bahrain detentions = CONFIRMED ACTION (Bahrain MoI primary, multi-source); Qatar mediator emergence = REPORTED (Axios primary scoop, multi-corroborated); UAE air defense engagement = CONFIRMED multi-source (UAE Defense Ministry primary, 3 wounded confirmed); Saudi MoFA = CONFIRMED ABSENCE of statement on Doha strike at filing time

recent_signals.headline: SAUDI PUBLICLY DENIED WSJ SCOOP ON OFFENSIVE-AIRSPACE LIFT — OFFICIAL POSITION IS THAT AIRSPACE WAS NEVER OPENED FOR US OFFENSIVE OPERATIONS DURING PROJECT FREEDOM. Saudi officials briefed Washington's press on background while 'the Saudi government denies the story on the record.' Unnamed Saudi source via Al-Hadath: 'the Kingdom did not allow the use of its airspace to support offensive military operations,' Riyadh 'supporting de-escalation and Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement.' Iran International + Times of Israel note Saudi/Kuwait have lifted restrictions per US officials, but Saudi official line is now public denial on the record. MBS playbook: operational deniability + public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track. The 'Saudi formally named primary mediator (or co-mediator) within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)' watchpoint is now within reach: Saudi has publicly endorsed Pakistan mediation = soft-mediator posture. Tadawul +0.7% May 8 (Friday partial trading day; Sunday open will be first reaction to Al-Hadath denial).
recent_signals.verification: Saudi public denial via Al-Hadath = CONFIRMED DIPLOMATIC SIGNAL on the record (Türkiye Today citing Al-Hadath primary); WSJ underlying scoop = CONFIRMED REPORTED via wire; US-officials lift narrative = REPORTED via background; Tadawul price = CONFIRMED yfinance

recent_signals.headline: TADAWUL -0.5% ONLY MAJOR EQUITY INDEX IN RED ON GLOBAL RISK-ON DAY — peace dividend BEARISH for war's primary fiscal beneficiary. Aramco fell 4.7% after-hours on UAE OPEC exit news; compounds Brent $107→$101 round-trip. ADNOC can undercut Saudi pricing (Murban discount potentially widening to $3.50/bbl by Q3 — WoodMac). Saudi break-even ~$95/bbl; World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 threaten fiscal runway. MBS Day 9 of 14 silence on UAE OPEC exit AND May 4 Iran-Fujairah strike. Chatham House: 'strategic recalibration' framing. UAE foreign minister rather than ruler at Jeddah summit — deepening diplomatic distance.
sources: Axios 'Year that shook the Gulf' May 2
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED Tadawul -0.5% (yfinance multi-source); CONFIRMED Aramco after-hours -4.7% on UAE OPEC exit; CONFIRMED MBS silence pattern via institutional analysis (Chatham House, Atlantic Council); REPORTED ADNOC Murban discount projection (WoodMac base case)

historical_summaries.summary: 6 signals. Saudi missiles intercepted | Saudi revived ~50% of oil exports via Yanbu Red Sea bypass | Iran attacked Yanbu port with drone, briefly halting loadings

24 signals. WaPo national security desk: Gulf allies privately urging Trump to continue fighting 'until Iran is decisively defeated | Saudi Arabia expelled Iranian military staff (persona non grata), citing Article 51 self-defense and UNSC Resolution 2817 | Saudi expelled Iranian military staff — persona non grata, citing Article 51 self-defense — effectively reversing the 2023 Beijing Agreement
